title: 11 LibPros scholars
primary_topics: liberty and prosperity under the rule of law
sub_topics: FLP scholarship eligibility criteria (top 10% class, 83% average, full load, dean commendation, essay, screening)
FLP dissertation contest (P300K champion, Ayala Group cosponsor)
FLP Makati head office (First Philippine Holdings, Lopez Holdings, Meralco, PLDT, MPIC, San Miguel)
Sereno as board chair / guest speaker

stances.claim: The board of judges was so impressed it recommended 11 instead of the planned 10 inaugural LibPros scholars — and the FLP happily agreed to expand the inaugural batch.

The FLP was organized in December 2011 during his 75th birthday to perpetuate the legal philosophy of safeguarding liberty and nurturing prosperity under the rule of law — pursuing the intertwining relationship of law and business, regulation and entrepreneurship.

Justice and jobs, freedom and food, ethics and economics, democracy and development, liberty and prosperity must always go together; one is useless without the other — attainment requires keen understanding of the intertwining relationship of law and business, regulation and entrepreneurship.

institutions: University of the Philippines (Fort Bonifacio Taguig campus)
events: Joan Largo's fourth professorial lecture in Cebu City (December 9, 2016)

signature_phrases: justice and jobs, freedom and food, ethics and economics, democracy and development, nay, liberty and prosperity
one is useless without the other
